Compromised axios (1.14.1/0.30.4) installed plain-crypto-js RAT payload. March 31, 2026 supply chain attack. Check package-lock.json for plain-crypto-js or axios 1.14.1/0.30.4
Vidar+GhostSocks dropper disguised as Claude Code installer. Rust binary. Legitimate Claude Code does NOT ship a .exe. Also distributed as TradeAI.exe. MD5: 77c73bd5e7625b7f691bc00a1b561a0f
Malicious archive lure from March 2026 campaign. Also seen as ClaudeCode_x64.7z, claude-cowork-win-x64.7z. Contains Rust dropper delivering Vidar v18.7 + GhostSocks
GhostSocks C2 server from Claude Code/OpenClaw malware lure campaign (March 2026). Also: 94.228.161.88:443
Vidar v18.7 C2 resolver via Steam profile. Also: telegram.me/g1n3sss. Non-browser processes accessing these from dev workstations are IOC
Empty stubs squatting internal Anthropic package names (@anthropic-ai/* namespace). Publisher: pacifier136. Dependency confusion risk
Trojanized repos: github.com/leaked-claude-code/leaked-claude-code, github.com/my3jie/leaked-claude-code, github.com/idbzoomh1. Same threat actor rotating AI brand lures since Feb 2026
Huntress March 2026: Bing search for 'OpenClaw Windows' redirected to fake installers on GitHub. Same Vidar+GhostSocks dual payload (TradeAI.exe). Same threat actor as Claude Code lures
OpenClaw default WebSocket gateway port. Outbound or listening on 18789 from endpoints indicates local AI agent framework
ZeroClaw default gateway port. Outbound or listening on 42617 from endpoints indicates local AI agent framework
Ollama default REST API port. Exposed to network is a misconfiguration indicator. Internal use indicates shadow AI model hosting
LM Studio default local server port (OpenAI-compatible API). Indicates local LLM hosting on developer workstation
